question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: dbWaiter(CommonProperties)] Can an unprivileged user set `hiero.mirror.common.databaseStartup.connectTimeout` to 1 second and `hiero.mirror.common.databaseStartup.interval` to 500ms so that `DatabaseWaiter` rapidly exhausts its retry budget against a slow database, causing the application to throw `IllegalStateException` and restart in a state where fee schedule data is not yet loaded?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: dbWaiter(CommonProperties)] Can an unprivileged user inject `hiero.mirror.common.databaseStartup.socketTimeout=1` so that `DatabaseWaiter.waitForDatabase()` sets a 1-second socket timeout in `jdbcProperties`, causing fee schedule reads that take longer than 1 second to be aborted and the application to fall back to stale fee data?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: spelHelper()] Can an unprivileged user craft a SpEL expression that calls `spelHelper.hashCode(byte[])` with fee-related byte arrays (e.g., serialized fee schedule protobuf) to leak hash values that enable collision attacks against fee schedule file integrity checks?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: spelHelper()] Can an unprivileged user inject a SpEL expression via a query parameter or request body that invokes `spelHelper.isNullOrEmpty()` on a fee-related collection, causing fee validation logic to treat a non-empty custom fee list as empty and skip fee enforcement?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: spelHelper()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `@Bean('spelHelper')` named bean registration to override the `SpelHelper` instance with a malicious subclass that returns `true` from `isNullOrEmpty()` for all fee collections, causing all custom fee schedules to appear empty and bypassing fee collection?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: spelHelper()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `hashCode(Object[])` method in `SpelHelper` via a SpEL injection vector to compute hash collisions for fee schedule file identifiers, enabling cache poisoning attacks where a malicious fee schedule is served in place of the legitimate one?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: CommonConfiguration (class-level @ConfigurationPropertiesScan)] Can an unprivileged user inject a rogue `@ConfigurationProperties` class under the `org.hiero.mirror` package scan path that overrides fee-related properties (e.g., `hiero.mirror.common.realm`) before `CommonProperties` is initialized, causing `SystemEntity` to resolve fee file entity IDs incorrectly?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: CommonConfiguration (class-level @ConfigurationPropertiesScan)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the broad `@ConfigurationPropertiesScan('org.hiero.mirror')` scan to register a properties class that binds to `hiero.mirror.common.databaseStartup.*` and sets `enabled=false`, bypassing the database readiness check and allowing fee schedule reads before the database is fully initialized?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: CommonConfiguration (class-level @EnableConfigurationProperties)] Can an unprivileged user supply environment variables that override `CommonProperties` fields (e.g., `HIERO_MIRROR_COMMON_REALM=99999`) to shift all fee-related entity ID calculations, causing the mirror node to read fee schedule data from a non-existent or attacker-controlled entity?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: CommonConfiguration (class-level @EntityScan)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `@EntityScan('org.hiero.mirror.common.domain')` annotation to introduce a rogue JPA entity class that maps to the `custom_fee` or `assessed_custom_fee` table with altered column mappings, causing Hibernate to read or write fee amounts with incorrect field bindings?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: CommonConfiguration (class-level @Configuration(proxyBeanMethods = false))] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `proxyBeanMethods = false` setting to bypass Spring's CGLIB proxy, allowing direct instantiation of `CommonConfiguration` beans (e.g., `systemEntity`) with attacker-supplied `CommonProperties` that have manipulated `realm`/`shard` values affecting fee entity ID resolution?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: systemEntity(CommonProperties)] Can an unprivileged user set `hiero.mirror.common.realm` to a value that causes `networkAdminFeeAccount()` (entity 98) to resolve to the same EntityId as `treasuryAccount()` (entity 2), causing fee-related transactions to be misattributed to the treasury and bypassing fee collection accounting?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: systemEntity(CommonProperties)] Can an unprivileged user set `hiero.mirror.common.realm` to a value that makes `networkPropertyFile()` (entity 121) resolve to the same ID as `exchangeRateFile()` (entity 112), causing the exchange rate used in fee calculations to be read from the network property file and producing incorrect HBAR-to-USD fee conversions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: CommonProperties.getInstance()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `IllegalStateException` thrown by `CommonProperties.getInstance()` when called before Spring context initialization to trigger an application restart loop, causing the fee schedule to never be loaded and all fee-dependent operations to fail?
